requirements.name: GOVERN – Organisational Context and Risk Management
requirements.description: Establish and monitor the organisation's cybersecurity risk management strategy, expectations, and policy. Covers: organisational context (GV.OC), risk management strategy (GV.RM), roles and responsibilities (GV.RR), policies and procedures (GV.PO), oversight (GV.OV), and cybersecurity supply chain risk (GV.SC).

requirements.name: IDENTIFY – Asset Management and Risk Assessment
requirements.description: Develop an organisational understanding to manage cybersecurity risk to systems, people, assets, data and capabilities. Covers: asset management (ID.AM) – inventory of hardware, software, data; risk assessment (ID.RA) – identifying, analysing and prioritising risks; improvement (ID.IM) – lessons learned.

requirements.name: PROTECT – Safeguards and Controls
requirements.description: Develop and implement appropriate safeguards to ensure delivery of services. Covers: identity management and access control (PR.AA) – authentication, authorisation, least privilege; awareness and training (PR.AT); data security (PR.DS) – encryption, backup, data handling; platform security (PR.PS) – hardening, patching, secure configuration; technology infrastructure resilience (PR.IR).

requirements.name: DETECT – Continuous Monitoring and Anomalies
requirements.description: Develop and implement appropriate activities to identify the occurrence of a cybersecurity event. Covers: continuous monitoring (DE.CM) – monitoring networks, computing hardware, software, service providers, and external threats; adverse event analysis (DE.AE) – detecting anomalies, investigating events, estimating impact.

requirements.name: RESPOND – Incident Response
requirements.description: Develop and implement appropriate activities to take action regarding a detected cybersecurity incident. Covers: incident management (RS.MA) – incident response plan, triage; incident analysis (RS.AN) – forensics, root cause; incident response reporting and communication (RS.CO) – internal and external communication, regulatory notification; incident mitigation (RS.MI).

requirements.name: RECOVER – Recovery Planning and Improvements
requirements.description: Develop and implement appropriate activities to maintain plans for resilience and to restore any capabilities or services that were impaired due to a cybersecurity incident. Covers: incident recovery plan execution (RC.RP) – recovery time objectives, restore from backup; incident recovery communication (RC.CO) – stakeholder updates, reputational repair.
